name: American Debt Counseling of Mi
description_short: Michigan-based non-profit debt counseling firm offering free budget analysis and debt management services across three locations with 38+ years of experience.
description_long:
American Debt Counseling of Michigan is an established credit counseling organization operating as a licensed and bonded debt management firm serving the state of Michigan. The company has maintained operations for over 38 years, building a long track record in the debt counseling space. They operate three physical office locations in Grand Rapids, Kalamazoo, and the Detroit Area, making in-person appointments accessible to residents across the state.

The company offers comprehensive debt management and credit counseling services without upfront fees. Their core offering begins with a free budget analysis appointment, available both in-person and by phone with experienced Michigan-based counselors. Beyond initial assessment, they provide debt management programs, budget creation assistance, credit report review, and negotiation support with creditors. They also handle specialized debt issues including tax liabilities, delinquent student loans, car repossession prevention, judgments and garnishments, housing delinquency, utility delinquency, creditor harassment complaints, and payday loan problems.

What distinguishes American Debt Counseling of Michigan is their local, in-person presence combined with their multi-decade tenure in the Michigan market. Unlike online-only services, they offer face-to-face appointments for people who prefer direct counseling relationships. Their licensed and bonded status provides regulatory oversight, and their extensive service menu suggests capability across diverse debt scenarios rather than a narrow specialty.

The primary caveat is limited information about outcomes, success rates, or program structures on their public website. While they emphasize affordability and working "solely for your benefit," specific details about fees (if any exist after the free consultation), debt reduction rates, or program completion percentages are not disclosed. Their website contains some minor inconsistencies (38 years vs. 30 years mentioned), suggesting outdated content that hasn't been recently refreshed. For consumers outside Michigan or those seeking quick online-only services, the location-dependent model may be restrictive.
pros: Free initial budget analysis appointment with no upfront costs
Licensed and bonded debt management firm providing regulatory credibility
Three physical office locations (Grand Rapids, Kalamazoo, Detroit area) for in-person counseling
38+ years of operating history and established presence in Michigan market
Comprehensive service scope covering 12+ debt types including tax liabilities, student loans, and creditor harassment
Phone and in-person appointment options for accessibility
Free resource available (7 tips for budgeting) for those exploring options
cons: Website lacks transparency on specific program fees, debt reduction percentages, or success rates
Service availability limited to Michigan only—not accessible to out-of-state consumers
Minimal information provided about debt management plan structures, creditor participation rates, or typical outcomes
Website contains inconsistencies (38 years vs. 30 years) suggesting outdated or poorly maintained content
No visible third-party reviews, certifications (like NFCC affiliation), or outcome data to verify claims
best_for: Michigan residents overwhelmed by multiple debts seeking in-person counseling and local accountability
Consumers dealing with specialized debt issues like tax liabilities, garnishments, or creditor harassment
People who prefer face-to-face appointments over online-only counseling relationships
Those with complex financial situations requiring comprehensive budget analysis and multi-creditor negotiation
diagnosis: Best for Michigan residents facing multiple debts who value in-person counseling and have 38+ years of local operating history to draw confidence from. Main caveat: limited public transparency on actual outcomes, program costs post-consultation, or creditor participation rates makes it difficult to assess real effectiveness before committing to a plan.
services: Free budget analysis and financial assessment
Debt management program setup and administration
State and federal tax liability counseling
